classObjective: Identify line symmetry in the graph of a function and determine the intervals where a function is increasing, decreasing, or constant.
workedExamples: 3-5, Example 1 — Determine Line Symmetry in Function Graphs
3-5, Example 3 — Determine Where f(x) Is Increasing and/or Decreasing
warmUp: Does the graph of y = x² have line symmetry? If so, what line?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: line symmetry analysis; reading intervals left to right; increasing/decreasing/constant identification
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying line symmetry and determining function behavior intervals
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying symmetry and determining where functions increase, decrease, or stay constant
assessment: Exit ticket: identify any line symmetry and describe the increasing/decreasing intervals for a given graph
capReflection: How does reading a graph from left to right help you determine where it is increasing or decreasing?
